Bright regulatory lines for AI are inherently complicated because models are just a piece of the puzzle: harnesses can make models more capable, a less capable open system may be more or less riskier than a more capable closed one, skills/connected systems change risk levels, etc

And AI systems may never be jailbreak-proof or hallucination free. And individual queries may matter less than a bad actor breaking a problem down into pieces and feeding it through multiple projects and prompts. And AIs themselves may change behavior unpredictably with context.
Very clever. And matches what I would expect: models are weak on vision relative to everything else, so visual steps are where errors accumulate most in workflows.

A studio charges thousands for a 10-second product film like this. A 3D artist, a render farm, a few days of work.

I made it from a single photo in minutes. Free.

The tool is Google's Gemini Omni. I ran three product-ad tricks through it. All three, with the exact prompts, https://t.co/ht9fP2owgc

Gemini Omni turns one reference image into finished video. Any input, text, a photo, audio, into a clean 10-second clip.

Two things make it work for product ads. The physics are real, so metal and glass stop looking floaty. And you edit by talking to it, one change at a time,

Trick 1 made the clip above. The Hero Orbit.

One product photo in, an Apple-style orbit ad out. No set, no crew, no turntable.

Prompt:
"Create a 10-second 16:9 cinematic product showcase in one continuous shot. [Product] rests on a glossy dark concrete floor. Camera slowly

Trick 2: the Impossible Shot.

Glass un-shattering off the floor. Water flowing upward. A building folding like origami. Omni's physics make the impossible look filmed, not rendered.

Prompt:
"Create a 10-second 16:9 cinematic video of impossible physics. [Glass reassembling / https://t.co/fceubAo3ao

Trick 3: the Exploded View.

Your product blows apart into floating components, gears spinning mid-air, then reassembles into a hero shot with room for your tagline. The one that stops the scroll.

I put all three full prompts plus seven more into a free Gemini Omni guide. Reply
